questions.question: How can you delete a block in Notion?

options.explanation: While right-clicking is a valid method for some content, it is not the only way. Other methods include using the block handle (⋮⋮) or the keyboard shortcut.

options.text: Use the ⋮⋮ icon in the left margin
options.explanation: The ⋮⋮ icon is a valid method, but it is not the only one. You can also use the ••• icon, right-click, or the keyboard shortcut.
You can delete blocks using the ⋮⋮ icon, the ••• icon, right-click, or by selecting content and hitting the delete key.

options.text: Select the block and press the Backspace key
options.explanation: While Backspace can delete pages, the delete key is the standard shortcut for deleting blocks.

questions.question: How often is a new version of a page recorded while you are actively editing it?

options.explanation: Versions are recorded more frequently than this. The interval is every 10 minutes while editing.
Notion saves a new version every 10 minutes as you actively edit a page. Another version is saved 2 minutes after you stop editing.
Notion automatically saves versions without requiring a manual save action.
Versions are saved much more frequently than once per hour.

questions.question: How long do pages remain in Trash by default before they are permanently deleted?

options.explanation: Version history retention is 7 days for Free plans, but Trash retention is 30 days.
This is not the default retention period for Trash.
By default, pages stay in Trash for 30 days before they are permanently deleted.
90 days is the retention period for version history on Business plans, not Trash.

questions.question: Where is the Trash located on mobile devices?

options.text: At the bottom of the left sidebar
options.explanation: The Trash is not located at the bottom of the sidebar on mobile; it is hidden in the top-left menu.
On both iOS and Android, Trash is located in the ••• menu in the top left of the sidebar.
Trash is a separate section in the sidebar, not located under the Settings menu.
Version history is a different feature and does not contain the Trash.

questions.question: What happens when you restore a database in Notion?

options.text: All properties and pages are restored, but the contents inside the pages are not
options.explanation: Restoring a database restores its pages and properties, but the text or content inside those individual pages must be restored separately.

options.text: Everything is restored, including the contents inside the pages
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The contents of the pages are not automatically restored when the database is restored.

options.text: Only the views are restored, but the data is lost
options.explanation: This is incorrect. The database structure (properties and pages) is restored, but the page contents are not.

options.text: Nothing happens, as you cannot restore a database
options.explanation: Databases can be restored, but with the specific limitations mentioned in option A.

questions.question: What is the block duplication rate limit?

options.explanation: The limit is higher than 10,000 blocks per hour.
You can only duplicate 20,000 blocks per hour. If you hit this limit, you will see a 'Please try again later' error.
The limit is 20,000 blocks per hour, not 100,000.
There is a hard limit of 20,000 blocks per hour.

questions.question: How can you view deleted properties in a database?

options.text: Go to the main database view and look for a 'Deleted' tab
options.explanation: Deleted properties are not in the main view; they are in a specific sub-section of the properties settings.

options.text: Go to Settings > Edit Properties > Deleted Properties
options.explanation: You can view, restore, or permanently delete deleted properties by scrolling down to the 'Deleted Properties' section in Edit Properties.
Version history tracks changes to content, not deleted database properties.

options.text: Deleted properties are permanently gone immediately
options.explanation: Deleted properties can be viewed and restored for a time before they are permanently deleted.

questions.question: What is the retention period for page version history on the Business Plan?

options.explanation: 7 days is the retention period for Free plans.
30 days is the retention period for Plus plans.
Business Plan users can view and restore page history for 90 days.
The text specifies 90 days for Business plans; Enterprise plans have unlimited retention.

questions.question: How do you access version history on mobile?

options.text: Click the 🕘 icon at the top of the page
options.explanation: While 🕘 shows revisions, the path to access version history is specific.
On mobile, tap the ••• icon at the top of the page and select 'Version history' to see the list of versions.

options.text: Go to Trash and select Version history
options.explanation: Version history is not located in the Trash.

options.text: Version history is not available on mobile
options.explanation: Version history is available on mobile, though the interface is slightly different from desktop.

questions.question: What is the limit on viewing version history if you are on a Free Plan?

options.explanation: Unlimited is only available on Enterprise plans.
30 days is available on Plus plans.
Free plan users can view and restore page history for 7 days.
A limited history is available on Free plans.

questions.question: What happens to a page once it has been permanently deleted from Trash?

options.text: It is immediately inaccessible to the owner
options.explanation: There is a grace period of 30 days where the data is retained even for workspace owners.

options.text: It becomes inaccessible to all users, even workspace owners
options.explanation: Once permanently deleted from Trash, the page is retained for 30 days before becoming inaccessible to everyone, including workspace owners.

options.text: It is moved to the Version History
options.explanation: Version history tracks edits, not permanently deleted items.

options.text: It can be restored by contacting support
options.explanation: Once permanently deleted (not just moved to trash), it is gone forever unless restored within the 30-day retention window.

questions.question: Is there a way to empty the Trash all at once?

options.text: Yes, by selecting 'Empty Trash' in the sidebar
options.explanation: There is no 'Empty Trash' button or option to clear the entire bin at once.

options.text: Yes, if you are on the Enterprise Plan
options.explanation: Even on Enterprise, there is no bulk empty feature; you must delete pages individually.

options.text: No, you must delete pages individually
options.explanation: The article explicitly states: 'There isn’t a way to empty your trash all at once.'

options.text: Yes, by right-clicking Trash and selecting Empty
options.explanation: Right-clicking Trash does not offer an empty option.

questions.question: Which icon allows you to jump to a specific past version in the browser?

options.explanation: The ⋮⋮ icon is used to delete or move blocks, not to access version history.
The 🕘 icon opens the dropdown menu of all revisions and comments.
Clicking the 🕓 icon next to a revision allows you to jump to that specific version in a new window.
The ••• icon opens the page menu, including Version history.

questions.question: How can you recover only specific content blocks from a previous version?

options.text: Open the version and click the 'Restore' button
options.explanation: Clicking 'Restore' reverts the entire page to that version. To get specific blocks, you must copy and paste them.

options.text: Copy and paste the blocks into your current page
options.explanation: The article states: 'To recover only specific content blocks... click on a certain version... copy those blocks, and paste them into your current page.'

options.text: Go to Trash and restore the blocks
options.explanation: Blocks are not stored in Trash; only pages are.

options.text: It is not possible to recover specific blocks
options.explanation: It is possible by copying and pasting from the old version.
